name: Container image with cleartext cloud keys granting high privileges
description: A container image contains hardcoded cloud credentials (AWS access keys, GCP service account keys) that grant high privileges, enabling lateral movement if the container is compromised
conditions: container_definitions CONTAINS 'AWS_ACCESS_KEY' OR container_definitions CONTAINS 'AWS_SECRET' OR container_definitions CONTAINS 'GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS'
remediation: 1. Remove hardcoded credentials from container images. 2. Use IAM roles for tasks (ECS) or workload identity (GKE). 3. Rotate any exposed credentials immediately. 4. Scan images for secrets before deployment.
